lumina_rules.description: LUMINA Framework Rules - Core principles that govern system behavior

rule.statement: When creating any Top N list, ALWAYS include opposites to double the list size
rule.formula: Top N → Top 2N (N positive + N negative)
rule.rationale: Half of the full spectrum damages the positive - provides complete picture

example.display: Still called 'Top 10' but includes both polarities

implementation.skillset: CORE - Full-spectrum analysis is Marvin's specialty
implementation.why_marvin: Marvin sees both sides - the positive AND the damaging. Because verification requires complete understanding.

rule.statement: Reuse old logic, apply to new situations
rule.formula: Pattern from Domain A → Apply to Domain B
rule.rationale: Proven patterns from other domains can solve current challenges

implementation.skillset: EXTRAPOLATION - Making unknown known through pattern application

rule.statement: Always target the Goldilocks zone - not too hot, not too cold
rule.rationale: Operating at extremes causes failures; optimal is sustainable

rule.statement: Documentation beats everything in order of precedence
rule.rationale: Without documentation, nothing else matters - knowledge is lost

rule.statement: Operate at 50% capacity until DYNO readiness checklist is complete
rule.formula: Current mode = PERFORMANCE_TUNING until all checklist items GREEN
rule.rationale: Prevent ECONNRESET errors and IDE instability during optimization

rule_categories.analysis: Rules governing how analysis is performed
rule_categories.pattern: Rules for pattern recognition and reuse
rule_categories.performance: Rules for capacity and performance management
rule_categories.priority: Rules for conflict resolution and precedence

enforcement.automatic: Some rules are enforced automatically by systems
enforcement.audit: All rules can be audited via @MARVIN verification
